text.title: Mobile Design Rules
text.summary: Touch target sizing, thumb zone rules, navigation pattern selection, form best practices, gesture guidelines, and accessibility requirements.

section.title: Touch Targets

rule.statement: Minimum touch target: 44x44pt (iOS) or 48x48dp (Android). Minimum spacing between targets: 8px. Apply to all interactive elements including links, icons, and checkboxes.
rule.rationale: Human finger pad is 40-60px wide. Targets smaller than 44pt produce 30-50% higher error rates on touch input. Insufficient spacing causes adjacent-target mis-taps even when individual targets meet the minimum size.

section.reference: Playwright script detecting touch targets below threshold at iPhone 13 viewport.

rule.statement: Choose navigation pattern based on section count: bottom tab bar for 3-5 main sections; hamburger menu only for many infrequently-accessed sections.
rule.rationale: Bottom tab bar is the highest-discoverability mobile navigation pattern. Hamburger menus hide navigation and increase task completion time by 2-3 steps for any navigation action.

list.item: Bottom tab bar: 3-5 main sections with roughly equal usage frequency.
Hamburger menu: many sections where most are rarely accessed.
Tab bar: 2-5 content types within one section.
Gesture navigation: supplement to visible controls, never the sole means of navigation.

rule.statement: Set the correct HTML input type for every form field. Use inputmode and autocomplete attributes to trigger the right keyboard and enable autofill.
rule.rationale: Wrong keyboard type (e.g., alphabetic for phone number) is the highest-leverage 5-minute mobile fix. Users must dismiss and reopen keyboards when the wrong type appears, which inflates form abandonment by 10-20%.

list.item: Minimize required fields to the absolute minimum for the task.
Enable autofill via correct autocomplete attributes.
Show/hide password toggle on password fields.
Clear, inline error messages immediately after invalid input.

rule.statement: Always provide a visible alternative for every gesture. Never rely on a gesture as the sole way to trigger an action.
rule.rationale: Gestures are invisible affordances. Users who do not discover them fall back to alternative paths or abandon the task. Discoverability requires a visible control paired with an optional gesture shortcut.

list.item: Tap: select or activate — always the primary interaction.
Swipe horizontal: navigate or reveal actions — pair with visible navigation controls.
Pull down: refresh — pair with a visible refresh button.
Pinch: zoom — provide visible zoom controls for accessibility.

rule.statement: Respect prefers-reduced-motion media query on all animations. Flag components missing this handling in code review.
rule.rationale: Vestibular disorders affect 35% of adults over 40. Animations that ignore reduced-motion preferences cause dizziness and nausea, making the product unusable for a significant subset of mobile users.

list.item: Touch targets: 44x44pt minimum (doubles as accessibility requirement).
Screen reader: VoiceOver (iOS) and TalkBack (Android) support required.
Text size: respect system font-size settings; do not force fixed sizes.
Color contrast: WCAG AA minimum (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large).
